themes: model competition (Kimi K2.6, Qwen-3.6, Elephant Alpha)

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Sharp take that Claude Cowork at $20/month structurally undercuts Tableau, Looker, and Retool — directly relevant to product strategy and pricing displacement.

worthClicking.author: @gdb
worthClicking.why: Greg Brockman announces Chronicle — Codex now watches your screen and builds persistent memory, making memory the new moat in coding agents.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Concise rebuttal to OpenAI's 'Anthropic has no compute' narrative — 8.5GW locked up across Amazon and Google in months.

worthClicking.author: @emollick
worthClicking.why: Rigorous study rerun with agentic AI shows Claude Code and Codex land near human median with far tighter variance — real evidence for AI research scalability pitch.

worthClicking.author: @kimmonismus
worthClicking.why: Sergey Brin personally driving Google's agentic coding strike team against Anthropic is a meaningful competitive signal.

worthClicking.author: @aakashgupta
worthClicking.why: Best available thread on Tim Cook's exit and what Apple's AI failure means for the post-iPhone era — 220K views, worth reading the full thread.

worthClicking.author: @kimmonismus
worthClicking.why: Kimi K2.6 benchmark drop — 58.6% SWE Bench Pro, 66.7% Terminal Bench, open weights — the clearest data point that Chinese open-source is now a real Claude Code rival.

worthClicking.author: @RoundtableSpace
worthClicking.why: Points to a full guide on building and selling Claude plugins — directly relevant to indie dev distribution.

worthClicking.author: @andrewchen
worthClicking.why: a16z Speedrun 2026 applications open today — $1M investments, 70+ companies, July-October in SF — actionable for indie founders.

voices.why: Consistently sharp on AI product strategy implications — the Cowork/Tableau displacement and Anthropic compute threads are the best analysis in this feed.
Brings actual research to AI capability claims; the agentic economist rerun study is exactly the kind of evidence useful for sales enablement.
Fast aggregator on competitive model releases and infra deals — Kimi K2.6 and Google DeepMind strike team posts were first-mover.
Direct Codex product announcements from inside OpenAI — Chronicle memory layer is strategically important.

examples: travel diary / lifestyle updates (airport selfies, sparkling water polls)
crypto shilling and BTC price posts
newsletter self-promotion spam (aakashgupta posting the same subscribe link 5+ times)
